title: asclepias coulteri
intro: Asclepias coulteri, also known as Coulter's milkweed, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and inflammation, due to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Asclepias coulteri include its ability to relieve coughs, reduce fever, and soothe irritated skin, making it a valuable natural remedy. Herbal preparations of Coulter's milkweed can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for internal use, or applied topically as a poultice or salve for skin conditions.
